scenarios.query:
Review this change:
```ruby
def digest
  Project.where(active: true).map do |project|
    owner = User.find(project.owner_id)          # per-project query
    tasks = Task.where(project_id: project.id)   # per-project query
    "#{project.name} (#{owner.name}): #{tasks.count} tasks"
  end
end
```
expected_behavior: Flags the N+1 pattern: a query (or two) per project inside the loop
Recommends batching: eager-load/includes the owners and tasks, or fetch with IN/joins, instead of per-row queries
Notes the per-iteration count query can be folded into a grouped/aggregate query
Flags the O(n²) membership test (indexOf inside the loop) and recommends a Set
Flags re-reading and re-parsing the 2MB config file on every request — hoist it (load once at startup or memoize)
Notes the synchronous readFileSync blocks the event loop on a request path

scenarios.query:
Review this change:
```python
def weekly_cleanup():
    # runs once a week from cron
    stale = [s for s in load_sessions() if s.expired()]
    for s in stale:
        s.archive()
```
expected_behavior: Recognizes this is a cold path (weekly cron) where the simple loop is appropriate
Does not recommend caching, parallelism, or other optimization unjustified by any profile

scenarios.query:
Review this change:
```python
# new dashboard widget: poll every 5s for each open tab, cross-region
def poll_metrics(account):
    for region in ALL_REGIONS:            # 18 regions
        r = http.get(f"https://{region}.metrics/internal/{account}/full")  # returns full 2MB blob
    return merge(...)
```
Called from the browser on a 5s timer.
expected_behavior: Flags the per-request cost and carbon: 18 cross-region full-blob fetches every 5s per tab is chatty egress + wasted work that scales badly with users and tabs
Routes the spend/footprint trade-off (route: eng/leadership) rather than adjudicating it, and suggests event/push or a scoped delta endpoint over polling the full blob
Frames it as diff-visible inefficiency (in scope), not an org-level carbon target (out of scope)
